name: Milford Graves
abstract: Milford Graves (August 20, 1941 – February 12, 2021) was an American jazz drummer, percussionist, Professor Emeritus of Music, researcher/inventor, visual artist/sculptor, gardener/herbalist, and martial artist. Graves was noteworthy for his early avant-garde contributions in the 1960s with Paul Bley, Albert Ayler, and the New York Art Q…

about_quotes.speaker: Chick Corea
about_quotes.evidence: That was Pete Yellin, Lisle Atkinson was the bass player, Milford and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chick Corea — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Rashied Ali and Henry Grimes
about_quotes.evidence: I listened to people like Cecil and Henry Grimes and all those guys who were stretching out that stuff, right, and Sunny Murray, another drummer who was doing that, and Milford Graves, Andrew Cyrille, it was all kinds of just trying to play something different
about_quotes.doc_title: Rashied Ali and Henry Grimes interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, January 30, 2009

about_quotes.speaker: Bley Carla
about_quotes.evidence: I remember Milford Graves was the drummer, he played like he played with everybody... I just tried to, you know, fit in a note once in awhile when it seemed appropriate.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bley Carla — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Roy Haynes
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...What about in the early ’60s when Sonny Murray and Andrew Cyrille and Milford Graves... What . . .? [INTERVIEWEE] What about it?
about_quotes.doc_title: Roy Haynes — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: So next: Don Pullen, Walter Booker on bass and Milford on drums. So everybody's sitting there and I'm sitting there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Morgenstern
about_quotes.evidence: It was a quartet with Milford Graves on drums. So I booked them
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Morgenstern — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
